title: GitHub App / installation-token exposure — rotation, scope minimization, octo-sts short-lived exchange
description: A platform engineer adopts GitHub App authentication for IaC (the recommended Hulumi default for the `pulumi-github` provider). Recent incidents — OpenAI Codex token-stealing branch-name injection (patched February 2026) and the Vercel April 2026 compromise — have demonstrated that App installation tokens with broad org-admin permissions create blast-radius equivalent to (or worse than) classic PATs. Hulumi recommends App tokens scoped to the minimum set of permissions required for IaC, rotated on a deterministic cadence; the `octo-sts/app` operational dependency offers OIDC-exchanged short-lived tokens as a defense-in-depth layer.
actors: Platform Engineer (trusted, installs the IaC GitHub App)
GitHub App Maintainer (trusted; could be compromised — Vercel April 2026 pattern)
External Attacker (untrusted, exfils a leaked installation token)
Compromised CI runner (semi-trusted; if a token reaches a runner, it can be re-used elsewhere)
assets: GitHub App installation token (short-lived, ~1h TTL by default)
GitHub App private key (long-lived; manufactures installation tokens)
App permissions surface (which org-level / repo-level scopes the App has)
Pulumi state and secrets that the App-authenticated `pulumi up` writes / reads

stride.name: Installation token leaked via runner logs or stack traces
stride.description: The IaC `pulumi up` runs on a CI runner; an error log emits an HTTP error response that includes `Authorization: Bearer ghs_...` echoed back from a 401 response. The CI logs ship to a log-aggregation tier with broader access than the IaC role; an SRE who shouldn't have IaC creds can now confirm the App token's prefix. Mitigation: token-redaction layer in `OrgFoundation`'s audit-event emitter (per Hulumi-for-GitHub critique S2: strip `/ghs_|ghp_|github_pat_|gho_|ghu_|Bearer\s+\S+/g` before any audit row is emitted).

stride.name: App private-key compromise → arbitrary installation tokens
stride.description: The App's private key is exfiltrated (via the Vercel April 2026 pattern: a compromised maintainer or a misconfigured secret store). The attacker manufactures arbitrary installation tokens for any org that has installed the App. Mitigation: store the private key in a hardware-backed KMS; rotate on a deterministic cadence; emit a `security_event.app_private_key_rotation_due` audit row when rotation is overdue.

stride.name: Over-broad App permissions → privilege escalation on token leak
stride.description: The IaC App was installed with `org-admin: write` for convenience (a one-time bootstrap need that became permanent). Any leaked installation token now grants org-admin powers beyond the IaC need. Mitigation: scope-minimize App permissions to the IaC actual use; track the permission set in `OrgFoundationOutputs` and emit drift signals when permissions widen; document `octo-sts/app` as a short-lived-token alternative for elevated-permission operations.

stride.name: App impersonation via OIDC misconfiguration
stride.description: The IaC App's installation token is fetched from a GitHub Actions workflow assuming a cloud role via OIDC. If the OIDC trust policy uses wildcard `sub` (UNC6426 pattern), an attacker-controlled workflow in the same org can mint tokens that look IaC-originated. Mitigation: see `github-oidc-trust-cloud-account` scenario; `G_OIDC_1` enforces three-axis safe `sub` declaratively.

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/baseline.github.OrgFoundation
recommendedComponents.rationale: Disables classic-PAT auth at startup-hardened tier (`disableClassicPats: true`); ships token-redaction layer in audit-event emitter (per critique S2).

recommendedComponents.name: @hulumi/policies.github.HulumiGithubHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.rationale: H1–H4 enforce hardened defaults declaratively at preview-time; reject configurations that would broaden App permission scope without an explicit `Suppression` justification.

recommendedComponents.name: octo-sts/app
recommendedComponents.rationale: STS-style OIDC token exchange replaces classic PATs and long-lived installation tokens with short-lived tokens. Recommended companion auth pattern; do not vendor.
